purpose: Post-sync drift detection for reusable template-canonical governance surfaces.

notes: This manifest tracks reusable exact-match surfaces only.
Mixed surfaces are intentionally excluded because downstream projects may customize them.
The manifest does not include itself to avoid recursive self-hashing.

include_when: plus GOV-013 deliverables coord/scripts/check-template-sync.sh and coord/scripts/check-template-sync.test.js

enforcement.exact: Missing files or checksum drift fail the check.
enforcement.advisory: Reserved for future mixed-surface monitoring; advisory drift warns by default and fails under --strict.

items.rationale: Runtime authority hook wiring and public enforcement-boundary documentation.

AGENT-COMMAND / claim-orchestration surface (agents list/register/enable/disable, the agentid resolver + payload formatting, the claim/claimTicket/claimAgent/claimAgentSession cluster, resumeTicket, releaseAgent, rebindAgent, resolveHumanAdminOverride, detectCwdTicketClaimHazard, and the agent-status report builder) extracted from lifecycle.js as a DI factory (COORD-087, Wave 4 slice 3). The COMMAND LAYER above the governance-session.js session engine: it injects the session/registry readers+writers, identity resolution, owner-lease metadata (PROVIDER_REGISTRY), board readers, the mutation/lock wrappers and the journal appender rather than duplicating session semantics; findLockForTicket/getLockFiles and the journal/board binds are injected as deferred wrappers; wired in lifecycle.js after createGovernanceSession.

Module-owned agent-command behavior tests (claim transfer override, the same-owner other-thread owner-lease gate, agentid resolver payloads, agent-status release candidates, the cwd-claim hazard, rebind --fresh) relocated from governance.test.js (COORD-087, Wave 4 slice 3).

Architecture/complexity guardrails (QGATE-004, COORD-078): reusable, zero-dependency static-check library AND single-source policy for the arch gate. Exported pure checks (size/complexity/imports/duplication/monolith/hardcoding/deadcode) return the structured finding shape consumed by COORD-083's ticket generator; WARNING-FIRST defaults + config-driven thresholds; classify CLI shelled from the template gate.sh runners on full/ci. QSCAN-001 added two warning-first detectors: hardcoding (low-noise config-seam-leak: inline paths, repeated multi-word string literals, opt-in magic numbers; obvious-OK literals filtered) and deadcode (dispatch/facade/export-aware unreferenced-def detector that prefers false negatives and never auto-fails; subtree scans take a referenceRoot so cross-subtree references keep a def live).

Module-owned architecture-gate tests (COORD-078): each check fires on a positive fixture and passes a clean one, threshold/severity classification, WARNING-FIRST non-blocking default, escalation-to-fail, the importable finding-shape contract for COORD-083, and template gate.sh runner integration (arch absent on default, warning-first on full). QSCAN-001 adds detector tests for hardcoding (path fires; URL/module-specifier/prose silent; repeated multi-word string fires while single occurrence + shared enum/flag vocabulary stay silent; number whitelist) and deadcode (true-orphan fires; bare-identifier/export/__testing-facade/dispatch-string-key/cross-subtree references all keep a def live; severity:off toggle).

Dependency/security audit threshold policy (QGATE-002, COORD-076): single-source pass/warn/fail classification + npm-audit parsing + classify CLI consumed by the template gate.sh runners on the full/ci lanes.

Module-owned dependency-audit policy tests (COORD-076): threshold classification, graceful no-lockfile skip, and template gate.sh runner integration (audit absent on default, fail/warn/config-driven on full).

Board-rebuild-from-journal surface (rebuildBoardFromJournal, terminalJournalStatusForTicket, collectTicketsWithJournalDrift) extracted from lifecycle.js as a DI factory (COORD-091, Wave 4 residual). Replays the governance event journal to repair board/tasks.json rows whose Status/Owner drifted from the journal's terminal succeeded event (GOV-012); writes the board only inside the injected withGovernanceMutation envelope and injects readGovernanceEventLog/readBoard/getTicketRef/writeBoard/fail rather than owning wiring. Wired in lifecycle.js after createJournal and createGovernanceBoardState; the factory binds are re-destructured back so the dispatch/commands/__testing facade keys and cli.js's rebuild-board route are unchanged.

Behavior tests for the board-rebuild-from-journal surface (board-rebuild.js): the four GOV-012 rebuildBoardFromJournal assertions (regressed-row repair, idempotency, missing-row failure, --all best-effort) relocated out of governance.test.js when the journal-replay repair layer was extracted from lifecycle.js (COORD-091). They reach the surface through the stable governance.js __testing facade (which re-exports the createBoardRebuild factory binds) via the temp BOARD_PATH/GOVERNANCE_EVENT_LOG_PATH/QUESTIONS_PATH override seam.
Extracted reusable governance engine module; downstream templates must receive this exact control-plane behavior.
Template sync checker entrypoint created by GOV-013 for downstream drift detection.
Focused regression test for the template sync checker created by GOV-013.
CLI router/flag surface including production/runtime evidence receipt verbs and --receipt-result parsing.

Ticket closeout / land surface (finalize/land/finish-ticket/prepareDoneCloseout plus the closeout plan-update builders) extracted from lifecycle.js as a DI factory. Injects the ticket-transitions surface (moveReview/markDone/applyMarkDone) and provides prepareDoneCloseout back to transitions; wired after createTicketTransitions.
Module-owned closeout tests (buildNoPrCloseoutPlanUpdate fulfilled-by vs no-pr) relocated from governance.test.js.

ENT-010 conformance attestation (COMMUNITY per-team tier): derives a deterministic engine-integrity subject (engine version, TEMPLATE_SYNC_MANIFEST.json fingerprint, gate config + latest gate-artifact result/coverage/audit per COORD-080, RELEASE_PROVENANCE donor SHA per COORD-044 when present, and the ENT-002 journal chain head), signs the canonical digest with a LOCAL ed25519 keypair (private key gitignored under coord/.runtime/conformance-keys/), and re-verifies it (signature + drift/tamper). DI-factory wired in lifecycle.js, exercised via gov conform --attest / --verify-attestation. READ-ONLY except writing the attestation artifact + (first-run) the local keypair. This is the per-team self-verify and the exact input the Enterprise central re-hash service (ENT-007) would later verify.

ENT-010 unit tests for the conformance attestation module: emit produces a well-formed signed attestation with the 5 engine-integrity inputs; self-verify passes on a clean attestation; tampered subject/signature and drifted engine inputs are detected; identical inputs yield a deterministic digest; the private signing key is gitignored. Temp fixtures only — no network, no live engine/journal side effects.

COORD-107: the ENT conformance / engine-integrity CLI verb surface (gov conform ENT-002 journal hash-chain self-verify + ENT-010 signed conformance attestation emit/verify, and gov verify-engine ENT-011 engine version-pin + drift-check) extracted from lifecycle.js to get it back under the documented honest 5000 logical-LOC composition-root ceiling by EXTRACTION (not a budget bump): lifecycle.js dropped 5036 -> 4911. DI-factory wired in lifecycle.js — the createConformanceAttestation + createEnginePin CREATORS, the journal verifyGovernanceChain, the GovernanceError fail, and COORD_DIR are all injected; conform/verifyEngine are destructured back out for the commands dispatch map + cli.js case. Pure presentation glue over the two injected factories; behavior byte-identical (covered by the existing CLI-level gov conform tests in journal.test.js plus the conformance-attestation.test.js / engine-pin.test.js factory unit tests). READ-ONLY except writing conform --attest's gitignored attestation artifact + first-run keypair and verify-engine --pin's committed coord/engine-pin.json.

CI-safe, path-independent contract policy (CONTRACT-002, COORD-082): single-sources how a frontend repo RESOLVES the backend OpenAPI artifact (config-driven via coord/project.config.js repos.<F>.contract + repo-registry repoRoots, NOT a hardcoded sibling path), the deterministic OpenAPI->client codegen, and the staleness check (regenerate + diff) that FAILS when the committed generated client drifts. CLI (gen|check) shelled from the frontend contract:gen/contract:check scripts and the template gate.sh runner on full/ci.

Module-owned contract-policy tests (COORD-082): proves the OpenAPI source path resolves via config + repoRoots (not a hardcoded sibling), deterministic codegen, contract:check passes when current and FAILS when the source contract changes without regenerating, and graceful-skip when no contract block / no source artifact. Temp fixtures only.
Product-facing `coord` CLI executable wrapper (COORD-116) over coord-cli.js; mirrors the `gov` engine wrapper. Reusable product entrypoint plumbing.

Product `coord` CLI subcommand dispatcher (COORD-116): extensible command registry (init + conformance + upgrade as of COORD-118) with a pure-ish `dispatch` for routing/help/unknown-command. Distinct from the `gov`/cli.js engine verb surface.

Module-owned tests for the `coord` CLI dispatcher (routing/help/unknown-command) and `coord init` (fresh-dir create, idempotent re-run, --dry-run, existing-config preservation) using os.tmpdir scratch dirs.

`coord conformance` (COORD-117): product-facing packaging command that COMPOSES the existing conformance engine (conformance-verbs.js conform/verifyEngine + conformance-attestation.js + engine-pin.js) into one fail-closed entrypoint (--json/--attest/--verify). Zero crypto/conformance reimplementation; DI-factory so tests inject the engine + an ephemeral keypair.

Module-owned tests for `coord conformance` (COORD-117): pass/fail exit codes, --json, an --attest/--verify round-trip over an EPHEMERAL in-test ed25519 keypair, tamper detection, and a wrapper-digest==engine-digest proof that the command reuses the engine. os.tmpdir scratch coordDir; no committed keys.

Canonical CLEAN STARTER BOARD shape (SETUP-001/SAMPLE-001) — single source of truth reused by both `coord init` and the public release builder's clean-board step (COORD-116) so init and the release cut never disagree.

`coord init` (COORD-116): idempotent no-clobber zero->governed-board bootstrap for a target repo (scaffold project.config.js seam, seed starter board if absent, ensure product spec stubs). DI-factory; --dry-run writes nothing; never overwrites existing files.

App-local read-only coord-ui launcher wired by create-concord and concord init as `npm run coord-ui`; resolves the scaffolded repo root from coord/scripts, points the UI at that repo's coord/, and avoids sending adopters back to the donor checkout.

`coord upgrade` (COORD-118): managed engine-upgrade automation. Applies a new engine surface (--from <dir|bundle>) into an adopter repo, re-pins via engine-pin.js, then verifies; idempotent, --dry-run, and exact byte-rollback on verify failure. Builds ON engine-pin.js (no fingerprint/verify reimplementation); only ever writes manifest-tracked reusable surface, never project-local files. DI-factory so tests inject fs/log/engine-pin.

Module-owned tests for `coord upgrade` (COORD-118): apply (update changed + add new surface file, regenerate engine-pin.json, real-engine verify pass), idempotent re-run no-op, --dry-run no-writes, forced-verify-failure exact byte-rollback (incl. removing added files), surface-only guarantee (board/project.config.js never touched), and dispatcher routing. os.tmpdir scratch source+target trees.
COORD-501 immutable channel resolver and archive containment for automatic customer upgrades.
COORD-501 tests for repository normalization, archive path containment, and release digests.

Test-coverage threshold policy (QGATE-003, COORD-077): single-source pass/warn/fail classification + Node --experimental-test-coverage report parsing + classify CLI consumed by the template gate.sh runners on the full/ci lanes.

Module-owned test-coverage policy tests (COORD-077): threshold classification (pass/warn/fail by %), graceful no-data skip, artifact written under coord/artifacts/gates/<repo>/, and template gate.sh runner integration (coverage absent on default, config-driven via GATE_COVERAGE_MIN on full).

Deploy-vs-PR gate-drift contract (COORD-079/QGATE-005): asserts the template deploy workflow (.github/workflows/deploy.yml.template) invokes the CANONICAL gate entrypoint (bash <repo>/scripts/gate.sh <lane> or gov gate --lane) on a deploy-strength lane (full|ci, never default), and proves a hand-rolled partial-command workflow FAILS the check. Single-sources the canonical-invocation expectation via governance-constants CANONICAL_GATE_ENTRYPOINTS + DEPLOY_GATE_LANES; exports checkDeployGateContract for a generated repo to reuse against its own deploy.yml so the deploy gate can never drift below the PR gate.

Module-owned behavior tests for the wired dispatch loop reference harness coord/scripts/dispatch.mjs (COORD-033), relocated verbatim from governance.test.js (COORD-096, residual split slice 1). They drive dispatch.mjs as an imported ESM module against a seeded manifest file — provider-agnostic, read-only, deterministic, no live board.

MUTATING governance repair/recovery surface (doctorFix behind `doctor --fix`, reconcileGovernance, recoverTicket, plus the private session-mirror rebuild helper) extracted from lifecycle.js as a DI factory. Wired after the journal/board-state factories; the read-only doctor diagnostic stays in lifecycle.js and delegates to the injected-back doctorFix only on --fix.
Module-owned doctor-recovery tests (the three doctor --fix repair-pass tests) relocated from governance.test.js.

READ-ONLY governance doctor REPORTING surface (resolveDoctorScope/resolveDoctorOwnerScope, the read-only doctor() report, and the buildCanonicalDerivedDriftError report builder) extracted from lifecycle.js as a DI factory (COORD-085, Wave 4 slice 1). Wired in lifecycle.js after createDoctorRecovery; injects doctorFix so the read-only doctor() still delegates to the MUTATING repair only on --fix without owning it.
Module-owned doctor-report tests (resolveDoctorScope/resolveDoctorOwnerScope, ticket-scoped resolution guidance, buildCanonicalDerivedDriftError) relocated from governance.test.js (COORD-085).

ENT-011 engine version-pin + drift-check (COMMUNITY per-team half of ENT-008; signing/registry authority is the Enterprise half ENT-008 and OUT OF SCOPE). DI-factory wired in lifecycle.js, exercised via gov verify-engine [--pin]. pin() snapshots the current engine surface (TEMPLATE_SYNC_MANIFEST manifest_version + its sha256 fingerprint, reusing ENT-010's conformance fingerprint, plus a per-file checksum snapshot of the exact-match files) into coord/engine-pin.json — the ONLY mutation. verify() is READ-ONLY: it re-derives the live surface and reports IN-SYNC or DRIFTED with the offending paths / whether the manifest fingerprint changed. Complementary to check-template-sync: that verifies internal manifest-vs-files consistency; this verifies drift from a FROZEN pinned version. NO signing.

ENT-011 unit tests for the engine version-pin + drift-check module: pin captures the current manifest fingerprint + version; verify reports IN-SYNC right after pin; a mutated tracked engine file / a mutated manifest / a missing tracked file are each detected as drift with the offending path; the fingerprint is deterministic; no-pin reports no_pin without crashing; verify is read-only (never mutates the pin file). Temp fixtures only — no network, no live manifest/engine side effects.
Fleet golden-path wrapper is reusable governance operator guidance.
Fleet golden-path wrapper tests are reusable governance validation.
Governance follow-up relation and readiness helpers are reusable control-plane runtime.

Module-owned behavior tests for followups.js (nextTicketId allocation, resolveFollowupPromptPath inheritance/coverage, applyFollowupRelation mapping) relocated out of governance.test.js (COORD-100, facade capstone).

Gate-artifact completeness schema + validator (QGATE-006, COORD-080): single-source REQUIRED_FIELDS (lane, commit, result, duration_ms, command_list, coverage, audit, artifact_paths) + validateGateArtifact + formatCompletenessSummary. Consumed by gate-runtime.js to measure/surface artifact completeness instead of silently synthesizing thin artifacts; mirrors audit-policy.js/coverage-policy.js (single-source the contract once in Node).

Module-owned gate-artifact completeness tests (COORD-080): validator verdicts (complete/missing/nullable-with-reason) plus template gate.sh runner integration proving the runners emit a complete artifact (real duration, commit, command list, embedded coverage/audit summaries) on default + full lanes.

COORD-092: gate PROCESS-orphan containment + provenance-scoped reaper — the third leg of orphan governance next to worktree cleanup and session-stub reaping. Owns the pidfile registry under coord/.runtime/gate-procs/, detectOrphans (read-only; wired into doctor-report) and reapOrphans (mutating; wired into doctor --fix and the gov reap-gate-procs verb). Reusable: kills ONLY recorded PIDs whose live /proc start-time still matches the recorded one (PID-reuse guard) — structurally never a process-name scan.

COORD-092: tests for the gate-proc registry/reaper — registry round-trip, trap teardown, orphan detection, recorded-PID-only kill, reused-PID refusal, and live-owner ignore. Fixtures only (injected kill spy + fake /proc); no real process is ever signaled except a test-spawned throwaway sleep.

Gate script/invocation/artifact-dir resolution, package-manager detection, and clean-checkout gate runner extracted from lifecycle.js. COORD-080 (QGATE-006): validates the emitted/synthesized gate artifact against gate-artifact-schema.js and records complete + incomplete_fields so artifact completeness is measured, not silently synthesized. COORD-093: finalizes duration_ms from a monotonic process.hrtime.bigint measurement around the gate spawn (sub-second, never negative) + clampDurationMs backstop so a bad runner-supplied value can never fail the schema.

Module-owned gate-runtime tests relocated from governance.test.js. COORD-080: asserts the bash synthesize-path artifact shape validates incomplete (duration_ms + command_list missing) via gate-artifact-schema. COORD-093: asserts clampDurationMs yields a schema-valid non-negative duration for a measurable run and defends the schema against negative/NaN/non-number durations.

Gate-lane vocabulary contract (COORD-075/QGATE-001): asserts the single source of truth GATE_LANES (default|full|ci) matches the template gate.sh runners, the gate-runtime validator, the MCP enum, BOOTSTRAP_CONTRACT, and the coord-ui lane list so the lane contract cannot silently drift.

Repo-gate ATTRIBUTION + board-record surface (add-repo-gate verb, classifyGateAttribution, formatRepoGateEntry) extracted from plan-command.js as a DI factory; kept separate from gate-runtime.js's execution surface (COORD-069). Records the audit (COORD-076), coverage (COORD-077), and architecture/complexity (COORD-078) gate signals as board-record annotations.

Module-owned repo-gate attribution/board-record tests relocated from plan-command.test.js (COORD-069); covers audit (COORD-076), coverage (COORD-077), and architecture/complexity (COORD-078) annotation encoding.

Shared raw git invocation: gitTry (tolerant primitive) plus generic-message runGit/gitOutput wrappers; single-sources the git -C <dir> argv shape + encoding policy de-duplicated from worktree-ops/gate-runtime/lifecycle/governance-validation/runtime-cleanup/board (COORD-072).
Current executable wrapper surface is reusable governance entrypoint plumbing.
Governance board read/write, row lookup, waiver/index, sync-trigger, and simple status/index reducer helpers are reusable control-plane runtime.

Shared governance constants (stalled-lock / template-feedback windows + trigger pattern), escapeRegExp, and the gate vocabulary single-sources: GATE_LANES (COORD-075/QGATE-001) plus the deploy-gate contract CANONICAL_GATE_ENTRYPOINTS + DEPLOY_GATE_LANES (COORD-079/QGATE-005) consumed by the deploy workflow template and deploy-gate-contract.test.js so deploy gates can never drift below the PR gate. De-duplicated from lifecycle.js and governance-repair.js (COORD-071).
Governance shared mutable context is reusable control-plane runtime.
Module-owned behavior test for governance-context.js (tryReclaimStaleDirectoryLock) relocated out of governance.test.js (COORD-100, facade capstone).
Governance MCP server surface including production/runtime evidence read tools and mutation-gated receipt writers.
Governance MCP tests including production/runtime evidence tool routing and mutation-gate coverage.

Governance PLAN-SHAPE service (scaffoldSelfReviewCycle/buildDefaultGovernancePlan/normalizeGovernancePlanShape/formatGovernancePlanEntry/formatGovernanceReviewProfileEntry/formatGovernanceRepairEntry/parseGovernancePlanEntries/buildScaffoldPlanRecord/ensurePlanStub) extracted from lifecycle.js as a DI factory (COORD-295, lifecycle decomposition slice #4 per the COORD-291 composition-root boundary contract). Behavior-preserving move of governance-plan normalization + scaffold-plan construction — distinct from plan-records.js (canonical plan record IO) and plan-command.js (the gov plan/update-plan verbs). CRITICAL: NO behavior change — plan JSON/markdown round-trips byte-stable where expected (normalizeGovernancePlanShape/parseGovernancePlanEntries/formatGovernancePlanEntry + the review-profile/repair sibling formatters produce byte-identical output) and buildDefaultGovernancePlan/buildScaffoldPlanRecord/ensurePlanStub/scaffoldSelfReviewCycle yield identical plan records. COORD-007: buildDefaultGovernancePlan seeds expected_closeout.base_ref from the LIVE REPO_INTEGRATION_BRANCHES map, injected BY REFERENCE (the __testing.paths.REPO_INTEGRATION_BRANCHES in-place mutation propagates). Primitives (state, REPO_INTEGRATION_BRANCHES, DEFAULT_INTEGRATION_BRANCH, isRepoBackedCode, repoNameForCode, toArray, the state-io canonical readers/writers) injected BY REFERENCE; the plan-record IO collaborators wired LATER by createPlanRecords (readPlanRecord/extractPlanBlock/renderPlanRecordBlock/appendPlanBlock/syncPlanRecordFromBlock/planRecordPath/writePlanCompatibilityBlockFromRecord) injected as deferred (...a)=>fn(...a) wrappers. Wired in lifecycle.js EARLY (before createGovernanceValidation/createPlanRecords); the nine names re-destructured back so the commands dispatch, the __testing facade (buildDefaultGovernancePlan/ensurePlanStub), and the deferred wrappers other factories inject resolve byte-identically. lifecycle.js 4473 -> 4261 raw LOC.

Behavior + DI wiring-guard coverage for the governance-plan-shape.js extraction (COORD-295). Exercises the factory directly with injected fake deps: the format/parse/normalize byte-stable round-trip (formatGovernancePlanEntry<->parseGovernancePlanEntries, the review-profile/repair sibling formatters, normalizeGovernancePlanShape repo-default collapse vs explicit-shape preservation), the scaffoldSelfReviewCycle deterministic raw line, and the buildScaffoldPlanRecord stable repo-backed record shape (4 vs 3 review cycles). The buildDefaultGovernancePlan (repo-default + COORD-006/007 REPO_INTEGRATION_BRANCHES live-mutation) tests and the ensurePlanStub canonical-record-seam tests (stale-stub no-overwrite + scaffold-record seeding) were relocated VERBATIM from lifecycle.test.js through the same fully-wired __testing facade (byte-identical assertions). Adds a DI wiring guard: the factory returns exactly the nine public functions, and lifecycle.js re-exports the two facade plan-shape helpers (buildDefaultGovernancePlan/ensurePlanStub, BRACKET-form, COORD-280 scanner-safe) unchanged.
Read-only governance-repair report/classification helpers (template-feedback alerts, question-queue, drift guidance, stale-lock) extracted from lifecycle.js.
Focused unit tests for governance-repair read-only helpers.

Governance agent/session registry, provider identity, liveness, session token, and lock identity helpers are reusable control-plane runtime. COORD-087 (Wave 4 slice 3) exports PROVIDER_REGISTRY from the factory so the extracted agent-commands.js command layer can inject the same provider metadata.

Module-owned lower-level session / identity / lock-authority ENGINE behavior tests, every subject defined in governance-session.js: registry/session read+legacy-migration (readAgentSessions/readAgentsRegistry), runtime session fingerprinting (runtimeSessionFingerprint), effective-thread-id resolution (resolveEffectiveThreadId, including the COORD_SESSION_ID override and the foreign-adoption/ambiguous-identity fail-closed cases), current-agent identity (ensureCurrentAgentIdentity no-touch), session-token minting + idle-stub reaping (getOrCreateSessionToken), mutation/repair ownership + guidance (assertTicketMutationOwnership, ensureTicketMutationOwnership, describeTicketMutationOwnershipIssue, assertTicketRepairOwnership, resolveOwnerIdentity), the pure lock/identity validators (isCompleteLockPayload, findDoingTicketForOwner/isDoingStatus, allocateAgentSimpleId), and the v2 owner-authoritative lock writer/rebinder (writeLock/rebindTicketLock). Relocated from governance.test.js (COORD-097, residual split slice 2). The agent-COMMAND orchestration layer stays in agent-commands.test.js; lifecycle-owned reaper/lock-integrity cases (reapIdleAutoClaimedProviderStubs, ensureDoingTicketLockIntegrity, buildStartOwnershipRaceMessage, refreshLockHead, assertCurrentTicketLockIntegrity) stay in governance.test.js.

Shared governance test utilities for module-split tests while preserving facade-based assertions. Hosts the COORD-003/004/transition workspace harness (createMinimalGovernanceWorkspace, setupCoord003Workspace, readBoardRow) shared by governance.test.js and ticket-transitions.test.js. Hosts withCanonicalTicketPrompt, lifted from governance.test.js so the staying facade prompt tests and the relocated token-economics context-pack tests share one definition (COORD-096).
Reusable adoption-friction governance command surface.
Regression coverage for reusable adoption-friction governance command surfaces.
Module-owned validation / readiness orchestrator. COORD-374 moved business-context and ADR readiness dimensions behind the pure GateCheck registry while preserving the exported validation facade.

Module-owned validation / readiness behavior tests, every subject defined in governance-validation.js: readiness evaluation (deriveGovernanceReadiness, collectStartReadinessBlockers, evaluateReadiness), review-plan readiness + closeout validation (collectReviewPlanReadinessIssues, assertReviewPlanReady, submitRequiresReviewPlanCheck), the testing-infrastructure and feature-proof audit derivers (deriveTestingInfrastructureAudit, deriveFeatureProofAudit, validateFeatureProofEntry), the no-PR / supersede landing validators (assertAlreadyLandedNoPrReconcileReady, assertLandingIntegrity, classifyLandingRecord, detectSupersedeLandingBypass), and the self-review-cycle helpers (replaceSelfReviewCycles, inferRequiredReviewRound). Relocated from governance.test.js (COORD-098, residual split slice 3). landing-resolution.test.js keeps commit/base-ref resolution and landing-audit.test.js keeps audit/report writers; lifecycle-owned validation (assertCommittedReviewState, appendReviewFollowupPlan, ensurePlanStub) and cross-module facade cases stay in governance.test.js.
Governance CLI facade is a template-canonical reusable control-plane executable entrypoint.

Governance test suite encodes reusable control-plane behavior and canonical runtime expectations. The landing-audit report/record/testing-infra-audit tests moved to landing-audit.test.js (COORD-070). The deep doctor-report behavior tests (resolveDoctorScope/resolveDoctorOwnerScope, ticket-scoped resolution guidance, buildCanonicalDerivedDriftError) moved to doctor-report.test.js (COORD-085). The deep buildTicketNextCommands guidance behavior tests moved to ticket-guidance.test.js (COORD-086, Wave 4 slice 2). The deep agent-command behavior tests (claim transfer, the same-owner other-thread owner-lease gate, agentid payloads, agent-status release candidates, the cwd-claim hazard, rebind --fresh) moved to agent-commands.test.js (COORD-087, Wave 4 slice 3). The deep landing commit-resolution behavior tests (resolveLandingBaseRef/resolvePrLandingBaseRef stale-local origin fallback, extractCommitShas, pickBestLandingCommit ancestry) moved to landing-resolution.test.js (COORD-088, Wave 4 slice 4). The four GOV-012 rebuildBoardFromJournal behavior tests moved to board-rebuild.test.js (COORD-091, Wave 4 residual). The deep token-economics behavior tests (COORD-026/027/028/029/030/031 cost-ledger, precheck, context-pack, tier-policy, plan-waves, dispatch-plan) moved to token-economics.test.js and the COORD-033 wired-dispatch reference-harness tests moved to dispatch.test.js (COORD-096, residual split slice 1). The lower-level session/identity/lock-authority engine behavior tests (readAgentSessions/readAgentsRegistry migration, getOrCreateSessionToken minting+reaping, runtimeSessionFingerprint, resolveEffectiveThreadId, ensureCurrentAgentIdentity, the assert/ensure/repair mutation-ownership + describeTicketMutationOwnershipIssue + resolveOwnerIdentity cluster, isCompleteLockPayload/findDoingTicketForOwner/allocateAgentSimpleId, and the v2 writeLock/rebindTicketLock owner-authority cases) moved to governance-session.test.js (COORD-097, residual split slice 2). Owns resolving a ticket's git context (repo root/branch/worktree/lock, lock-first with a worktree fallback) and the closeout PR-ref decision tree — distinct from landing-resolution.js (commit-ancestry/base-ref/source-commit resolution) and landing-audit.js (provenance landing records), which stay SEPARATE modules wired alongside it (296 sits beside them, does not absorb them). CRITICAL: NO behavior change — PR/no-PR evidence is byte-identical (the --no-pr X-lane and the PR-backed path resolve through the same resolveLifecyclePrRefs tree: explicit --pr refs -> existing board pr_index refs -> repo-backed branch PR discovery -> fail-closed; verifyPrEvidence INJECTED with allowNoPr:true, not reimplemented). ENT-005 OTLP exporter (COMMUNITY tier): reads the durable governance journal and emits it as zero-dependency, deterministic OpenTelemetry OTLP/JSON (tickets-as-traces, lifecycle-verbs-as-spans, cost/tier/attribution as span attributes; non-ticket events as log records). Hermetic — no live collector, no network.

Code-quality automation + periodic quality-ticket generator (COORD-083, QGATE capstone): runs the COORD-078 arch-checks library over a target repo, normalizes findings into proposed governed tickets with a STABLE per-finding key, dedups against open board tickets (via a [qkey:...] description marker), and (with --apply) files them through 'gov open-followup'. Safe-by-default: dry-run is the default; a per-run cap prevents board flooding; a severity floor limits filing. Exposed as the 'gov quality-scan' verb; schedulable via cron/CI per coord/product/QUALITY_AUTOMATION.md. QSCAN-001 wires the new arch-checks hardcoding/deadcode findings through the same proposal pipeline (stable qkey via stableDetail, title + fix-framing). COORD-286 adds --propose/--status proposed: files auto-debt as quarantined `proposed` via the COORD-285 single-writer create path `gov file-ticket --status proposed` (dedup counts proposed rows, so the cadence is idempotent); back-compat default stays `todo` via open-followup. Single-shot cadence runner: coord/scripts/quality-propose-cron.js.

Module-owned tests for COORD-083 quality-scan: stable cross-run finding key (ignores volatile measured values), severity->priority mapping, severity floor, DEDUP against open tickets (incl. done/superseded re-file + within-run collapse), per-run cap with no silent drop, the --apply filing path (mocked gov runner, no real board touched), and the dry-run-by-default CLI guarantee. QSCAN-001 adds stable-key + proposal-mapping coverage for the new hardcoding/deadcode checks. COORD-286 adds the --propose acceptance coverage: PROPOSE-FILES-PROPOSED (routes to file-ticket --status proposed), DEDUP-INCLUDES-PROPOSED (proposed rows count for idempotent dedup), BACKCOMPAT (default todo via open-followup), invalid --status rejection, and the single-shot quality-propose-cron runner.
